findings.evidence: `rg` hits: line 39 has `lemma weightedDegree_le_of_le {w : X → ℤ} (hw : ∀ x, 0 ≤ w x)`, while line 53 has the analogous local form `weightedDegree_lt_of_le_of_ne_of_pos_on_support ... (hw : ∀ x ∈ (E - D).support, 0 < w x)`; the proof at lines 61-64 already sums only over the difference support.
findings.fix: Make the pointwise lemma support-local, e.g. `weightedDegree_le_of_le_of_nonneg_on_support (hDE : D ≤ E) (hw : ∀ x ∈ (E - D).support, 0 ≤ w x)`, and derive the current global monotone wrapper from it.
findings.issue: `weightedDegree_le_of_le` assumes `∀ x, 0 ≤ w x`, but for fixed `D ≤ E` the proof only needs nonnegativity on `(E - D).support`; this is stronger than the nearby support-local strict lemma at lines 53-55.

summary: The pointwise weighted monotonicity lemma is stated with an unnecessarily global nonnegativity assumption; the bundled `Monotone` corollary is the right place for the global hypothesis.
